title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Federal Trade Commission relating to "Premerger Notification; Reporting and Waiting Period Requirements".
plain_summary: Congress is voting on whether to reject a rule made by the Federal Trade Commission. The rule is about what companies must do when they want to merge or combine with other companies. It tells companies what information they need to report and how long they have to wait. If Congress passes this resolution, the FTC rule will be canceled and will not be allowed to work.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress disapproves of a rule that the Federal Trade Commission made about companies reporting when they want to merge together and how long they must wait. This rule was called Premerger Notification; Reporting and Waiting Period Requirements and was published in the Federal Register on November twelfth, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced.
